notes: The operations structure documentation is described as delivered but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The menus include allergen-relevant items (smoked eel, Baltic herring, porcini, lamb, duck, pike perch, dairy, rye) and the à la carte and tasting menus are bilingual but no allergen labelling or EU FIC 1169/2011 compliance framework is mentioned anywhere in the documentation. At pre-opening stage, no allergen management system is confirmed in place.
description: Allergen information not available or EU FIC Regulation 1169/2011 requirements not met

notes: Operations documentation including 'minimum SOPs and daily routine structure' was delivered in early March but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' A cleaning schedule would be part of this documentation but its existence and adoption is unconfirmed. No specific mention of a cleaning schedule in the delivered materials.
description: No written cleaning schedule or existing schedule not followed consistently

notes: The operations documentation covers onboarding and SOPs but no specific mention of food hygiene certification requirements for the team. Chef candidates (Vidas and Andrius) have not yet started. No reference to Level 2 food hygiene certificates or equivalent Lithuanian food safety training requirements being tracked or required.
description: Team members lack Level 2 food hygiene certificates or refresher training overdue

notes: The operations structure documentation is described as covering 'minimum SOPs and daily routine structure' but no HACCP documentation is specifically mentioned. Given that the owners have not confirmed reviewing the operations documentation, and no HACCP system is referenced in the full description of deliverables, this is a significant pre-opening gap.
description: No documented HACCP-based food safety management system in place

notes: No internal food safety audit process is mentioned in the operations documentation deliverables. Given the pre-opening stage and the fact that the operations documentation has not been confirmed as reviewed, no self-assessment or audit cadence has been established.

notes: Recipe cards and portion standards are not mentioned in the operations documentation deliverables. The operations documentation covers 'minimum SOPs' but has not been confirmed as reviewed by owners.
description: No standardised recipes or portion control for food and drink items

notes: Menus have been developed as HTML files and delivered, but no mention of written recipe cards or drink specification standards being created for the team. The operations documentation covers SOPs but recipe standards are not listed among the deliverables. Chef candidates have not yet started, so no kitchen standards have been established.
description: No written recipe cards or drink specification standards for the team to follow

notes: An onboarding process is described as part of the operations documentation delivered in early March, but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The onboarding programme exists on paper but has not been validated, adopted, or tested. Chef candidates visit March 24 — no confirmed onboarding readiness.
description: No structured onboarding or induction programme for new team members

notes: No team meetings or briefing structure is described as being in place. The operations documentation covers 'daily routine structure' but this has not been confirmed as reviewed. No team exists yet to meet, but no briefing cadence has been established for when they do.
description: No regular team meetings briefings or communication forums

notes: The consultant (Matas Paulinas) is the single point of knowledge, coordination, and execution across brand, operations, vendor management, hiring, menus, website, and financial setup. 'You had to contact him [Vidas] yourself because the owners weren't sharing information.' 'You called him [Andrius] directly.' The entire pre-opening project is dependent on one external consultant with no internal operational counterpart confirmed. If Matas exits before handover, the project collapses.
description: One barista or manager holds disproportionate skill or customer relationships

notes: The owners (Simona and Rolandas) are not functioning as operational decision-makers. The consultant is executing everything: 'brand coordination, operations structuring, vendor management, staff hiring strategy, physical branding, menu development, onboarding documentation, financial setup coordination — essentially everything.' There is no evidence of the owners taking protected management or strategic time — rather, they are absent from the process entirely. Severity override: this is more severe than the signal's default — the owners are not working every shift, they are not working at all on the project.
description: Owner or founder working every shift with no protected management or strategy time

notes: No reference to food safety certification requirements being tracked for incoming staff. Chef candidates (Vidas and Andrius) have not yet started. The operations documentation does not specifically mention food safety certification as a hiring requirement. This is a pre-opening gap.
description: Required food safety certificates not held by all food handlers

notes: The operations documentation includes 'daily routine structure' and 'minimum SOPs' but these have not been confirmed as reviewed by the owners. No opening or closing checklist is specifically mentioned among the deliverables. Pre-opening gap.
description: No standardised opening or closing checklist for daily operations

notes: Menus have been developed with pricing (à la carte mains €24-28, tasting menu €105, wine pairing €65, glass range €7-12, bottles €34-95) but no mention of individual menu item costings or gross margin calculations being performed. The HR cost dynamics tool was built for salary ranges but no food or beverage cost modelling is mentioned among the deliverables.
description: Individual menu item costings not calculated and gross margins unknown per item

notes: No weekly or monthly financial review rhythm is mentioned. No financial reporting structure is confirmed in place.
description: No weekly or monthly financial review rhythm in place

notes: No cash flow forecasting or forward financial planning is mentioned. The DSS contract is unsigned, the accounting relationship is unconfirmed, and no budget or forecast document is referenced among the deliverables. The salary ranges question 'has been open since January.'

notes: No annual budget or financial forecast is mentioned among the deliverables. The HR cost dynamics tool addresses salary ranges but no revenue forecast, operating budget, or pre-opening cost plan is referenced. The DSS contract is unconfirmed.

The FOH manager posting strategy references 'personal profile post first, then HR groups, then Stories, then Kalvos accounts' but this is a hiring tactic, not a content strategy. 'All coded, all branded, all waiting for final photography and confirmed operational details.' This is a confirmed gap blocking both the website launch and physical menu production.

notes: No email list, newsletter, or direct customer communication channel is mentioned in the deliverables. The website has a 'reserve' section but no CRM or email capture mechanism is referenced.

notes: No customer acquisition strategy beyond the website and social media presence is mentioned. The venue is in a small town in Žemaitija — a destination concept that requires active outreach to drive awareness. No PR strategy, press outreach, influencer engagement, or launch campaign is referenced in the deliverables.

notes: No written business plan is mentioned among the deliverables. The consultant has built operational documentation, HR tools, and financial tools, but no strategic business plan document is referenced. The owners' vision is articulated conceptually but not in a formal business plan.
description: No written business plan or strategic direction document

notes: No team is in place yet, but the operations documentation covering standards and expected behaviours 'has not been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The owners cannot align a team on standards they have not themselves reviewed or adopted.

notes: The operations documentation includes job descriptions, responsibility and reporting logic, onboarding process, work discipline and communication model, minimum SOPs, and daily routine structure — but this is described as 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' A staff handbook may be embedded in this documentation but its existence as a standalone reference document is not confirmed.
description: No staff handbook or reference document for policies expectations and procedures

notes: The owner vision is articulated by the consultant ('slowness, nature, family, and grounding,' 'a place to arrive, not just to eat') but the owners themselves are not engaging with the operational documentation, not responding to emails, not attending agreed meetings, and have not reviewed the menus, SOPs, or hiring materials. 'Rolandas still hasn't looked at them [the menus] as of mid-March.' The vision exists in the consultant's documents but has not been shared with or validated by the team — because there is no team yet, and the owners are not present in the process.
description: Owner or founder has a vision for the cafe but has not shared it with the team

notes: The entire pre-opening project knowledge is held by the consultant (Matas Paulinas). The owners are not engaged with the deliverables. If the consultant exits, there is no documented handover protocol and the owners have not reviewed or internalised the brand, operations, hiring, or financial documentation. 'Nine deliverables are complete but many are waiting for someone to look at them.' This is an acute key-person dependency risk at the most critical project stage.
description: No protocol for managing knowledge transfer when key staff leave

The consultant is physically working on site ('you're physically at Kalvos, working on staff facilities') with no confirmed H&S documentation.
description: No documented health and safety risk assessment for the premises

notes: No fire risk assessment or fire safety compliance is mentioned in the deliverables. The 'technical inspection checklist' is listed as waiting for owner response but its content is not described. Pre-opening fire safety compliance is a legal requirement before opening.
description: Fire risk assessment not conducted fire extinguishers not serviced or escape routes blocked

notes: The bar menu includes alcohol (cocktails, craft beers, spirits, wine). No mention of an alcohol licence being obtained or in process. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. Licensing compliance is a pre-opening requirement.

notes: Employment contracts are not mentioned in the deliverables. The operations documentation covers job descriptions and responsibility logic but no employment contract templates or compliance with Lithuanian labour law is referenced. Chef candidates have not yet started.
description: Employment contracts minimum wage working time regulations not compliant

notes: No gas safety certificate or electrical installation condition report is mentioned. The property is a converted building with a restaurant, kitchen, and 9 hotel rooms. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. These are pre-opening legal requirements.
description: Gas safety certificate or electrical installation condition report overdue or absent

notes: The DSS contract with Audronė at Verslo Struktūra 'has been floating since February 26 and it's unclear if it's signed.' DSS in Lithuania handles VAT and tax reporting. Without a confirmed accounting and tax compliance partner, i.SAF VAT declaration compliance is at risk. This is a pre-opening legal requirement.
description: Monthly i.SAF VAT declaration not submitted to VMI by 20th of following month or i.VAZ goods movement reporting non-compliant

notes: No POS system is mentioned in the deliverables. Without a POS system integrated with Lithuanian tax authority electronic reporting requirements, the venue cannot legally operate. This is a pre-opening gap.
description: POS system not integrated with national tax authority electronic reporting requirements for audit trail and receipt generation

notes: The DSS contract is unconfirmed. No systematic process for tracking mandatory social insurance contributions or filing employer notifications for new hires is mentioned. Chef candidates are about to be hired with no confirmed HR compliance infrastructure.

description: Food business operator approval certificate not current or renewal process not tracked

notes: The bar menu includes alcohol (cocktails, craft beers on tap, spirits, wine). No alcohol licence is mentioned in the deliverables. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. An alcohol licence is a pre-opening legal requirement.

notes: Employment contracts are not mentioned in the deliverables. The salary ranges question 'has been open since January.' Chef candidates are about to be hired with no confirmed employment contract framework. Lithuanian labour law compliance is not referenced.

notes: The property has a garden and outdoor spaces. 'The garden measurements since February' are listed as a blocked open item. No outdoor terrace strategy, permits, or seasonal setup plan is mentioned. The garden measurements being unresolved since February suggests the outdoor space planning is stalled.
description: No strategy to maximise outdoor terrace revenue during summer season including permits setup procedures or seasonal menu

notes: The venue is positioned as a destination in Žemaičių Kalvarija — a pilgrimage town that attracts religious tourists, particularly in summer. No segmentation strategy between pilgrimage/religious tourists, destination diners, and local regulars is mentioned. The concept is built for a specific audience but no differentiated service or pricing approach for different visitor types is described.
description: No segmentation of tourist traffic versus local regulars with no differentiated service pricing or marketing approach
